findings.evidence: line 52 has `@[expose, implicit_reducible] noncomputable def fiberTorsor`; lines 65-160 provide the public characteristic API.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose, implicit_reducible]`; keep clients using `fiber_sdiv_eq`, `fiber_sdiv_smul`, and the `deckEquivFiber_*` lemmas instead of unfolding the structure.
findings.issue: `fiberTorsor` exposes its body with `@[expose, implicit_reducible]`, making the chosen `sdiv` implementation part of the public interface even though the file provides characteristic lemmas for it.

findings.evidence: line 65 marks `fiber_sdiv_eq` as simp; line 118 marks `deckEquivFiber_symm_eq_sdiv` as simp with the reverse equality.
findings.fix: Choose one simp normal form, preferably keep `deckEquivFiber_symm_eq_sdiv` as the old-API-to-torsor rewrite and remove `[simp]` from `fiber_sdiv_eq`.
findings.issue: `fiber_sdiv_eq` and `deckEquivFiber_symm_eq_sdiv` are both `@[simp]` but rewrite the same expression in opposite directions: `e₁ /ₛ e₂` to `(deckEquivFiber ... e₂).symm e₁` and back.

summary: The torsor API is mostly present, but the public surface leaks the implementation and installs conflicting simp normal forms.
